column1: # Format (3 fields, tab-separated — required by zap-baseline.py -c):
# url-regex: .* matches all URLs; use a specific pattern to scope the rule.
# Actions:   FAIL (fail scan), WARN (log, don't fail), PASS (suppress)
#            IGNORE (actively suppress regardless of baseline state)
#   FAIL  — genuine exploitable risk on this stack
#   WARN  — needs review; likely informational or false positive in lab context
#   PASS  — intentionally not addressed at this phase
#   IGNORE — ZAP client/scanner noise, not a server-side finding
# ── Injections (FAIL — these are the point of the lab) ──────────────────────
# PASS in local/CI context — no TLS termination in the DAST stack
# 10038 Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
# WARN only — CSP set by helmet; may still fire on 404 responses
# 10055 CSP — Failure to Define Directive with No Fallback
# 10024 Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
# 10025 Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in HTTP Referrer Header
# Source maps / build comments in dev build
# PASS — API uses JWT bearer auth (stateless), no session cookies, CSRF N/A
# PASS — DAST stack intentionally uses HTTP
# 10049 Storable and Cacheable Content — Cache-Control set by 404 handler
# 10063 Permissions-Policy Header Not Set — set explicitly in index.js
# This is a request header sent by browsers; ZAP's HTTP client doesn't send it.
# Not a server-side finding — suppress entirely.
